summary:
The patient has severe bleeding due to placenta praevia during labour leading to hypotension; while regional anaesthesia would typically reduce blood loss, the need for swift intervention favors using
General Anesthesia because it allows quicker stabilisation crucial under such emergency conditions. To ensure prompt surgical access and manage potential complications effectively, General Anaesthesia should be chosen instead of Regional one considering both maternal safety & fetal well-being are at stake urgently. Agent plans for General Anesthesia primarily driven by necessity for fast-acting measures against ongoing significant haemorrhagic event coupled with low BP indicating impending medical crisis necessitating timely interventions including controlled intubation and ventilation support immediately post induction without delay associated risks inherent within local/regional techniques when faced directly managing catastrophic obstetric emergencies involving active vaginal bleeds requiring expedient delivery via Cesarian Section procedure itself being time sensitive too thus making GA
